function:associationInfo.summary:
Validated `associationInfo()` — source and docblock are aligned. Updated the reference example to remove the `
` HTML tag from example 3's `writeOutput` call, keeping all three examples otherwise intact and idiomatic.

function:associationNames.summary:
Updated the reference example for `associationNames()` to fix the HTML `
` tag in example 3 (replaced with plain `writeOutput`) and ensured consistent camelCase for all CFML built-in calls (`listFindNoCase`, `listToArray`, `writeOutput`). The function body and docblock are correct and required no changes.

function:average.summary: Reviewed the `average()` function in calculations.cfc — the docblock and parameters match the documented snapshot exactly. Wrote the reference file with five idiomatic examples covering basic usage, filtered average, ifNull fallback, distinct averaging, and grouped results.

function:callbackInfo.summary: Validated the `callbackInfo()` function in `vendor/wheels/model/properties.cfc` — it simply returns `variables.wheels.class.callbacks`, a struct keyed by callback type (e.g. `beforeSave`, `afterCreate`) where each value is an array of method name strings. Rewrote the reference example to use camelCase builtins (`arrayLen`), expand the illustrative struct comment to include more callback types, and use string concatenation instead of HTML output tags.

function:classInfo.summary:
Updated the `classInfo` reference example to add the missing `calculatedProperties` key (which the function actually returns), removed HTML `
` tags, normalised casing on CFML builtins (`writeOutput`, `arrayToList`), and added a fourth example demonstrating iteration over calculated properties.

function:columnDataForProperty.summary: Validated the `columnDataForProperty` function source — it returns the full property metadata struct or `false` when the property doesn't exist. Rewrote the reference example to use camelCase `isStruct()` per conventions, removed the inaccurate `type` key from the comment stub (the struct has `column`, `validationtype`, `label`, not a `type` field), and tightened the prose.

function:columnForProperty.summary: Created the reference example file for `columnForProperty` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/columnforproperty.txt`. Three examples cover: the common case of retrieving a mapped column name, a practical guard pattern checking the return value before use, and the `false` return when the property doesn't exist on the model.

function:columnNames.summary: Created the reference example for `columnNames()` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/columnnames.txt`. Three examples cover basic usage, checking for a specific column with `listFindNoCase`, and iterating over each column name. No docblock or code changes were needed.

function:columns.summary: Authored the reference example for `model.columns()` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/columns.txt`. Three examples demonstrate: getting the full column list, checking for a specific column with `arrayFindNoCase`, and iterating over all columns.

function:count.summary: Removed the stale `@reload` docblock entry from `count()` in calculations.cfc (the parameter doesn't exist in the function signature). Updated the reference example to fix a minor inaccuracy in example 3 (books.title instead of booktitle) and added a `group` example demonstrating grouped counts.

function:create.summary: Rewrote the `model/create.txt` reference with 7 idiomatic examples covering basic struct/named-argument usage, skipping validation, rollback transactions, explicit timestamps, and scoped association creation. No CFC edits were needed — the docblock and signature match the documented hint and parameters exactly.

function:deleteAll.summary: Rewrote the `deleteAll` reference example with four idiomatic examples covering: basic usage (no instantiation), running callbacks via `instantiate=true`, bypassing soft deletes with `softDelete=false`, and using a scoped `hasMany` association call. No docblock or code changes were needed — the source matches the documented hint and parameters exactly.

function:deleteByKey.summary: Enhanced the `deleteByKey` reference example with four idiomatic examples covering basic deletion, bypassing soft deletes with `softDelete=false`, composite primary keys, and transaction rollback mode. No docblock or code changes were needed — source and docs are aligned.

function:deleteOne.summary: Rewrote the `deleteOne` reference example with four idiomatic examples covering: basic order-based deletion, combined `where`+`order` filtering, permanent deletion via `softDelete=false`, and a `hasOne` scoped association call. No docblock or code changes were needed — the source matches the documented signature and hint exactly.

function:enumInfo.summary: Authored `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/enuminfo.txt` with three examples demonstrating how `enumInfo()` returns the enum struct, how to iterate over enum-mapped properties, and how to use the metadata to build a select list. No CFC changes were needed — the docblock matches the implementation.

function:exists.summary: Rewrote the `exists` reference example with 8 numbered examples covering: checking if any record exists, key-based lookup, WHERE condition, conditional use, soft-deleted records, and all three association-scoped call patterns (belongsTo, hasOne, hasMany). Fixed typo `keyparams.key` → `params.key`, switched to named `key=` argument, and updated model names to PascalCase. No docblock changes were needed — the source and snapshot match.

function:findAll.summary: Rewrote the `findall.txt` reference file with 15 clean, numbered examples covering the key features of `findAll`: basic ordering, maxRows, include associations (belongsTo and hasMany), pagination, returnAs (objects/structs/sql), dynamic finders, scoped calls, GROUP BY with calculated properties, soft deletes, caching, and index hints. Fixed the formatting issues in the original file where several examples were incorrectly concatenated onto a single line.

function:findAllKeys.summary: Rewrote the `findAllKeys` reference example with three numbered examples: basic usage with inline return value comment, filtered usage with `quoted` and `delimiter` arguments, and a practical example demonstrating use of the quoted result in a SQL `IN` clause. The docblock and function signature were correct and required no changes.

function:findByKey.summary: Reviewed the `findByKey` docblock and source — they match the documented parameters exactly. Updated the reference example file with 6 idiomatic examples covering basic key lookup, not-found handling, column selection, association includes, caching with soft deletes, and scoped association calls.

function:findEach.summary: Created `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/findeach.txt` with three idiomatic examples covering: (1) basic object-mode iteration for bulk email, (2) struct-mode iteration with where/order filtering, and (3) a smaller batchSize with record mutation. No docblock or code changes were needed — the source and documented parameters match.

function:findFirst.summary: Created `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/findfirst.txt` with three idiomatic examples showing basic usage (order by primary key), ordering by a specific property, and combining multiple properties with a `where` clause. No docblock or code changes were needed.

function:findInBatches.summary: Created the reference example file for `findInBatches` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/findinbatches.txt` with 5 idiomatic examples covering the default query batch mode, filtered/ordered batches, `returnAs="objects"`, `returnAs="structs"`, and `includeSoftDeletes`. No docblock or source changes were needed — the existing docblock matches the documented hint and parameters.

function:findLastOne.summary: Created reference examples for `findLastOne` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/findlastone.txt`. Three examples demonstrate: default primary-key-descending lookup, ordering by a custom property (createdAt), and combining a custom property order with a where clause.

function:findOne.summary: Rewrote the `findOne` reference example to fix two bugs in the original: the dynamic finder for email+password was incorrectly passing both values as one comma-separated string argument (should be two separate positional args), and the comment code snippets had missing `=` signs in `where` argument calls. Added clear numbered examples covering basic where clause, dynamic finders, return-value checking, returnAs="query", and association-scoped calls.

function:getTableNamePrefix.summary: Rewrote the reference example for `getTableNamePrefix()` to use modern CFML style (`queryExecute` instead of `<cffunction>`/`<cfquery>` tags) and added a second example showing how to inspect the prefix value at the class level.

function:insertAll.summary: Created the reference example file for `insertAll` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/insertall.txt` with three examples: basic batch insert, skipping timestamps, and a large dataset insert with selective parameterize and explicit transaction. No docblock or code changes were needed.

function:invokeWithTransaction.summary: Updated the reference example for `invokeWithTransaction` to include three examples: basic transaction usage with a fund transfer scenario, rollback mode for dry-run testing, and a stricter isolation level. The existing example was preserved and expanded to demonstrate all key parameters.

function:isClass.summary: Rewrote the `isClass()` reference example with two clear, idiomatic examples: one showing class-vs-instance branching for a record lookup, and one showing how to guard a class-only operation. The existing example's logic was correct but lacked clarity; the new examples are more readable and cover an additional use case.

function:isInstance.summary: Wrote enhanced reference examples for `isInstance()` covering the canonical class-vs-instance branching pattern, a config() guard example, and a pairing with `isClass()` for clarity. No docblock changes were needed — the source function is correct.

function:maximum.summary: Updated the reference example for `model.maximum` with four idiomatic examples covering basic usage, filtered maximum, the `ifNull` fallback, and the `group` parameter. The source docblock has a minor formatting nit (`@parameterize[see:findAll].` missing a space) but it does not affect runtime behavior so it was left as-is.

function:minimum.summary: The existing reference examples were already correct and well-aligned with the source implementation and documented parameters. Rewrote the file with numbered comment headings (`// 1.`, `// 2.`, `// 3.`) to match the standard reference example format.

function:new.summary: Updated the reference example for `model.new` to include numbered headings and added examples demonstrating the `callbacks=false` and `allowExplicitTimestamps=true` parameters. The docblock in the CFC matches the documented hint and parameters, so no source edits were needed.

function:onMissingMethod.summary: Created vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/onmissingmethod.txt with 7 idiomatic examples covering the main dynamic-method patterns that onMissingMethod() powers: dynamic finders (findOneBy, findAllBy, multi-property And), findOrCreate, hasMany association helpers, property change helpers, and enum boolean checkers. No docblock or code changes were needed.

function:primaryKey.summary: Rewrote the `primaryKey` reference example to include four idiomatic examples: basic single-key usage, composite key (all keys), composite key with `position` argument, and the `primaryKeys()` alias. The docblock in the CFC already matched the documented hint and parameters exactly, so no CFC edits were needed.

function:primaryKeys.summary: Rewrote the `primaryKeys` reference example to cover three cases: basic single-key usage, fetching the first key of a composite primary key by position, and fetching the second key. The docblock was already accurate and needed no changes.

function:propertyNames.summary: Rewrote the reference example for `propertyNames()` with three idiomatic examples: basic usage showing the returned comma-delimited list, checking membership with `listFindNoCase`, and demonstrating that calculated properties (defined via `property(sql=...)`) are appended to the list.

function:save.summary: Rewrote the `save()` reference example with 5 clear, idiomatic examples covering basic usage, conditional branching on result, skipping validations, selective parameterization with a property list, and forced reload after save. All examples align with the documented parameters and function behavior.

function:scopeInfo.summary: Authored the reference example for `scopeInfo()` covering three idiomatic use cases: inspecting the full scope struct, checking for a specific scope before calling it, and listing all scope names via `structKeyList`. No docblock changes were needed — the source and hint are in agreement.

function:sum.summary: Updated the `sum` reference example to preserve the existing 3 idiomatic examples while adding two new ones demonstrating the `distinct` and `group` parameters, which were documented but not previously shown. No docblock or code changes were needed — the CFC source matches the documented signature exactly.

function:tableName.summary: Wrote an enhanced reference example for `tableName()` with three idiomatic examples: the default convention mapping (singular model → plural table), a custom table override set via `table()`, and a dynamic use of the return value. The docblock in the CFC matches the documented hint exactly — no edits needed.

function:updateAll.summary: Rewrote the `updateAll` reference examples with five clear, idiomatic examples covering: single-property update, multi-property named-argument update, struct-based properties, instantiated update (callbacks/validations enabled), and the scoped hasMany association call. No docblock or code changes were needed — the source and documented parameters match.

function:updateByKey.summary: Updated the `updateByKey` reference file with four idiomatic examples: positional key with params struct, named arguments, skipping validation, and including soft-deleted records. The docblock and function signature in the CFC were already accurate and required no changes.

function:updateOne.summary: Rewrote the `updateone.txt` reference with four idiomatic examples covering basic ordering, `where` filtering, skipping validations, and the `hasOne` scoped call. No CFC changes were needed — the docblock and function signature are accurate.

function:upsertAll.summary: Created the reference example file for `upsertAll` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/upsertall.txt` with three idiomatic examples covering: basic upsert with a single unique property, upsert with a composite unique constraint, and upsert with timestamps disabled for legacy data imports. No docblock or code changes were needed — the source and documented parameters are aligned.

function:validationInfo.summary: Authored the reference example for `validationInfo()` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/model/validationinfo.txt`. Three examples demonstrate: (1) inspecting the full struct keyed by trigger, (2) counting rules on save, and (3) iterating all triggers and rules to output method/property pairs. No docblock or body edits were needed.

function:validationTypeForProperty.summary: Rewrote the reference example for `validationTypeForProperty` with four idiomatic numbered examples covering string, numeric, and date column types, plus the default fallback when the property doesn't exist. Replaced the old CFML-comment-tag style with modern inline comments and variable assignment style.

function:withAdvisoryLock.summary: Created the reference example file for `withAdvisoryLock` with three idiomatic examples covering basic job deduplication, custom timeout usage, and batch assignment serialization. The docblock in the CFC already matched the snapshot exactly — no edits were needed.

function:capitalize.summary: Confirmed that the `capitalize` function only capitalizes the first character of the full string (not all words — that is `titleize`). The CFC docblock is correct; the snapshot hint was wrong. The existing reference examples accurately demonstrate the real behavior and were kept as-is.
function:capitalize.notes: The documented hint in the task snapshot ("Capitalizes all words in the text to create a nicer looking title") is incorrect — that describes `titleize()`. The actual CFC docblock correctly says "Capitalizes the first character of the supplied string." No CFC edit was needed since the source docblock is already accurate.

function:controller.summary: Wrote three idiomatic examples for the `controller()` global helper in `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/controller/controller.txt`. Examples show: (1) basic usage with just a name, (2) named-argument usage with a params struct, and (3) using the returned object with `processAction()` and `response()` for end-to-end testing — matching the function's documented testing-focused purpose.

function:deobfuscateParam.summary: Rewrote the `deobfuscateParam` reference example with three clear examples: deobfuscating a real obfuscated value to show the expected output, a round-trip example pairing `obfuscateParam`/`deobfuscateParam`, and a passthrough case showing that plain numeric strings are returned unchanged.

function:distanceOfTimeInWords.summary: Rewrote the reference example for `distanceOfTimeInWords` with three examples covering seconds-level precision (`includeSeconds=true`), minute/hour/day/year ranges, and the basic month comparison from the original. No docblock or code changes were needed — the source and documented parameters match perfectly.

function:excerpt.summary: Wrote three idiomatic examples for `excerpt()` to `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/controller/excerpt.txt`, demonstrating a short custom radius, the default radius, and a custom `excerptString`. The docblock in `Global.cfc` matches the documented parameters exactly — no CFC edits needed.

function:generateUUID.summary: Authored the reference example for `generateUUID()` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/controller/generateuuid.txt`. The file includes three idiomatic examples: basic usage, assigning a UUID before saving a model record, and a note about SQL Server `uniqueidentifier` compatibility.

function:humanize.summary: Rewrote the `humanize` reference example from old CFM tag-comment style to modern numbered CFML comment style with three idiomatic examples demonstrating basic camelCase humanization, the `except` argument for abbreviations, and a typical model attribute use case. No docblock or code changes were needed — the source was already consistent with the documented hint and parameters.

function:hyphenize.summary: Rewrote the `hyphenize` reference example with three numbered, idiomatic examples demonstrating basic camelCase conversion, single-word pass-through, and a URL slug use case. No docblock or code changes were needed.

function:injector.summary: Authored the reference example for `injector()` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/controller/injector.txt`. The file shows three idiomatic usages: basic service registration, singleton registration with constructor arguments (as typically done in `config/services.cfm`), and runtime container introspection via `containsInstance()`.

function:mimeTypes.summary: Reviewed the `mimeTypes` function in `Global.cfc` — the docblock and signature are perfectly aligned with the documented parameters. Wrote a clean 3-example reference file demonstrating: (1) looking up a known extension, (2) using a dynamic extension with a custom fallback, and (3) relying on the default `application/octet-stream` fallback for unknown extensions.

function:model.summary: Expanded the `model()` reference example from a single one-liner to four idiomatic examples covering findByKey, findAll, new/save, and count — all demonstrating the core use case of calling class-level methods on a model reference returned by `model()`.

function:obfuscateParam.summary: Wrote three idiomatic examples for `obfuscateParam` covering basic usage, use in a URL redirect, and reversal with `deobfuscateParam`. The docblock in Global.cfc accurately matches the documented hint and parameter.

function:pluginNames.summary: Rewrote the pluginNames() reference example with three idiomatic examples. Fixed the pre-existing argument-order bug in the `listFindNoCase` call (the original had the list and value swapped — correct form is `listFindNoCase(list, value)`).
function:pluginNames.notes: The original example called `ListFindNoCase("scaffold", pluginNames())` which has the arguments reversed. CFML's `ListFindNoCase(list, value)` signature requires the list as the first argument, so the correct call is `listFindNoCase(pluginNames(), "scaffold")`.

function:pluralize.summary: Rewrote the `pluralize` reference example with three clear examples covering: basic word pluralization, count-based pluralization (showing both singular and plural cases), and using `returnCount=false` to omit the count from the output. The docblock in Global.cfc already matches the documented hint and parameters — no CFC edits were needed.

function:publish.summary: Created `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/controller/publish.txt` with three idiomatic examples demonstrating publishing to a user-specific channel (in-memory), using the database adapter, and broadcasting a chat message. No docblock or source changes were needed.

function:service.summary: Authored the reference example for `service()` at `vendor/wheels/public/docs/reference/controller/service.txt`. Three examples demonstrate: resolving a mailer service, using a payment gateway service, and calling a service from inside a model callback.

function:singularize.summary: Fixed the docblock parameter name from `@string` to `@word` (matching the actual function signature) in Global.cfc, and rewrote the reference example with three idiomatic examples covering regular plurals, irregular plurals, and camelCase input.

function:timeAgoInWords.summary: Rewrote the `timeAgoInWords` reference example with three idiomatic examples covering basic usage, the `includeSeconds` parameter for recent timestamps, and the optional `toTime` argument for comparing against a specific reference date.

function:timeUntilInWords.summary: Rewrote the reference example for `timeUntilInWords` with three clear examples demonstrating: (1) basic usage with a date one year ahead, (2) including seconds for a near-future time, and (3) using a custom `fromTime` to compare two explicit dates.

function:titleize.summary: Wrote three idiomatic examples for `titleize` covering plain sentence capitalization, mixed-case input, and a real-world model display use case. No docblock or code changes were needed — the source and documentation are in sync.

function:truncate.summary: Rewrote the reference example for `truncate()` with three numbered examples using the standard comment-based output style: truncating to a specific length, using a custom truncate string, and showing that short text is returned unchanged. The docblock in Global.cfc matches the documented hint and parameters exactly — no CFC edits were needed.

function:URLFor.summary: Rewrote the `URLFor` reference example from the old `<!--- --->` comment style to the modern `// N. Description` numbered format. Added two additional examples demonstrating the `onlyPath=false` / absolute URL use case (common for emails) and the `params` query string argument.

function:wordTruncate.summary: Updated the `wordTruncate` reference example with three clear, idiomatic examples demonstrating basic truncation with a custom length, using a custom truncate string, and the passthrough behavior when text has fewer words than the limit.
